description_long: This is a small, single-story house with a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade features a modern, vertically-paneled door with a long frosted glass insert, flanked by large rectangular windows on either side, allowing ample natural light into the entryway. The lower portion of the exterior walls is clad in red brick, while the upper section is finished with light-colored wood paneling. The windows are symmetrically arranged, and the roof extends outward to form a modest overhang above the entry. The structure is compact and likely serves as a standalone residential unit or a small outbuilding. The overall design is simple, functional, and contemporary, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation.
description: A compact, single-story house with a gable roof, brick and wood exterior, large front windows, and a modern glass-paneled door.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door with a vertical glass panel, flanked by two large windows and brickwork at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable roof, brick base, and a pair of tall windows; entryway is not visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with a similar door and window arrangement as the front, but with a more enclosed appearance and visible roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gable roof, brick base, and a pair of tall windows; entryway is not visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Modern door with vertical glass panel, no steps, small overhang, and flanking windows
